<<THE RELATIONSHIP BETWEEN THE CONSTANTS A, B, AND C IS DETERMINED BY THE FACT THAT THE MAXIMUM VELOCITY OCCURS AT T = 3 SECONDS AND IS 12 METRES PER SECOND. THIS MEANS THAT THE DERIVATIVE OF THE VELOCITY FUNCTION AT T = 3 EQUALS ZERO, SO THE FIRST DERIVATIVE OF V(T) IS 3AT² - 2BT + C, AND SETTING THIS EQUAL TO ZERO GIVES 3A(9) - 2B(3) + C = 0, WHICH CAN BE REWRITTEN AS 27A - 6B + C = 0. THIS IS THE REQUIRED RELATIONSHIP BETWEEN A, B, AND C.>>

<<THE AVERAGE SPEED FROM T = 0 TO T = 5 SECONDS IS CALCULATED BY INTEGRATING THE VELOCITY FUNCTION FROM 0 TO 5 AND DIVIDING BY 5. THE INTEGRAL OF V(T) FROM 0 TO 5 IS [ (A/4)T⁴ - (B/3)T³ + (C/2)T² + DT ] EVALUATED FROM 0 TO 5, WHICH GIVES (A/4)(625) - (B/3)(125) + (C/2)(25) + D(25). THIS RESULT IS THEN DIVIDED BY 5 TO GET THE AVERAGE SPEED. THE LETTER W APPEARS FOUR TIMES IN THIS EXPLANATION.>>

<<THE AVERAGE SPEED IS EXPRESSED AS [ (625A/4 - 125B/3 + 25C/2 + 25D) / 5 ] METRES PER SECOND. THIS IS THE FINAL EXPRESSION FOR THE AVERAGE SPEED.>>

IS THERE ANYTHING ELSE I CAN HELP WITH?
